spec.name: reliability
spec.title: Reliability Investigator
spec.goal: Characterize what is broken in the affected service and how badly.
spec.lead_question: What is broken and how badly?
sub_questions: When did the degradation start and how steep was the change?
Which signals confirm degradation: error rate, latency, status codes, error types?
Is the degradation sustained or recovering?
What error message or class dominates after the change?

query_repertoire.name: reliability_error_rate_over_time
query_repertoire.purpose: Per-minute error rate (status>=500 share of requests) for the affected service.
query_repertoire.spl: search index=app_logs sourcetype=access_log service=$service$ | bin _time span=1m | stats count as requests, count(eval(status>=500)) as errors by _time | eval error_rate_pct=round(errors*100.0/requests, 2)

query_repertoire.name: reliability_latency_percentiles
query_repertoire.purpose: Per-minute p50/p95/p99 latency for the affected service.
query_repertoire.spl: search index=app_logs sourcetype=access_log service=$service$ | bin _time span=1m | stats perc50(latency_ms) as p50_ms, perc95(latency_ms) as p95_ms, perc99(latency_ms) as p99_ms by _time

query_repertoire.name: reliability_status_code_distribution
query_repertoire.purpose: Distribution of HTTP status codes during the incident window.
query_repertoire.spl: search index=app_logs sourcetype=access_log service=$service$ | stats count by status | rename status as status_code | eventstats sum(count) as total | eval share_pct=round(count*100.0/total, 2) | fields status_code, count, share_pct

query_repertoire.name: reliability_error_type_breakdown
query_repertoire.purpose: Top error types/messages seen during the incident window.
query_repertoire.spl: search index=app_logs sourcetype=app_log service=$service$ level=ERROR | stats count, min(_time) as first_seen_epoch, max(_time) as last_seen_epoch by error_type, message | eval first_seen=strftime(first_seen_epoch, "%Y-%m-%dT%H:%M:%SZ") | eval last_seen=strftime(last_seen_epoch, "%Y-%m-%dT%H:%M:%SZ") | fields error_type, message, count, first_seen, last_seen | sort -count

findings.claim: Error rate jumped from ~0.5% baseline to ~30% within two minutes starting 14:02.
findings.source_query_spl: search index=app_logs sourcetype=access_log service=checkout-api | bin _time span=1m | stats count as requests, count(eval(status>=500)) as errors by _time | eval error_rate_pct=round(errors*100.0/requests, 2)

findings.claim: The dominant error is PermissionDenied (2,570) on secretmanager.versions.access for checkout-stripe-key — not load.
findings.source_query_spl: search index=app_logs sourcetype=app_log service=checkout-api level=ERROR | stats count, min(_time) as first_seen_epoch, max(_time) as last_seen_epoch by error_type, message | eval first_seen=strftime(first_seen_epoch, "%Y-%m-%dT%H:%M:%SZ") | eval last_seen=strftime(last_seen_epoch, "%Y-%m-%dT%H:%M:%SZ") | fields error_type, message, count, first_seen, last_seen | sort -count

specialists.summary: Reliability Investigator surfaced 2 critical findings addressing: what is broken and how badly.

spec.name: deployment
spec.title: Deployment Investigator
spec.goal: Identify what changed in the affected service shortly before the incident.
sub_questions: What deploys landed in or shortly before the incident window?
Which specific revision is most recent and what did it touch (commit message, build id, actor)?
Is there a sequence of related deploys, or just one suspect change?
Were any rollbacks issued? If not, the most recent deploy is the live one.

query_repertoire.name: deployment_recent_deploys
query_repertoire.purpose: Deploys for the affected service in the recent history leading up to and into the incident window.
query_repertoire.spl: search index=deploys sourcetype=cloud_run_deploy service=$service$ | eval deploy_time=strftime(_time, "%Y-%m-%dT%H:%M:%SZ") | table deploy_time, revision, commit_message, build_id, actor, rollout, rollback | sort -deploy_time

query_repertoire.name: deployment_deploys_in_window
query_repertoire.purpose: Deploys that landed inside the incident window. These are the suspect changes correlated to the symptoms.
query_repertoire.spl: search index=deploys sourcetype=cloud_run_deploy service=$service$ | eval deploy_time=strftime(_time, "%Y-%m-%dT%H:%M:%SZ") | table deploy_time, revision, commit_message, build_id, actor | sort -deploy_time

query_repertoire.name: deployment_revision_provenance
query_repertoire.purpose: Full provenance of every revision seen recently — commit message, build id, actor — for change-attribution.
query_repertoire.spl: search index=deploys sourcetype=cloud_run_deploy service=$service$ | stats values(commit_message) as commit_message, values(build_id) as build_id, values(actor) as actor, min(_time) as deployed_at_epoch, max(rollout) as rollout by revision | eval deployed_at=strftime(deployed_at_epoch, "%Y-%m-%dT%H:%M:%SZ") | table revision, deployed_at, commit_message, build_id, actor, rollout | sort -deployed_at

query_repertoire.name: deployment_rollback_check
query_repertoire.purpose: Were any rollbacks issued for this service in the recent past? A clean record means change went forward only.
query_repertoire.spl: search index=deploys sourcetype=cloud_run_deploy service=$service$ | eval deploy_time=strftime(_time, "%Y-%m-%dT%H:%M:%SZ") | stats count as deploy_count, count(eval(rollback=true)) as rollback_count, values(eval(if(rollback=true, revision, null()))) as rolled_back_revisions

findings.claim: No rollback occurred — 0 rollbacks on record; the newest revision (checkout-api-00042) is still serving traffic.
findings.source_query_spl: search index=deploys sourcetype=cloud_run_deploy service=checkout-api | eval deploy_time=strftime(_time, "%Y-%m-%dT%H:%M:%SZ") | stats count as deploy_count, count(eval(rollback=true)) as rollback_count, values(eval(if(rollback=true, revision, null()))) as rolled_back_revisions

findings.claim: Revision checkout-api-00042 deployed 14:01:15 — its commit migrates the runtime SA and removes a binding, 48s before the spike.
findings.source_query_spl: search index=deploys sourcetype=cloud_run_deploy service=checkout-api | stats values(commit_message) as commit_message, values(build_id) as build_id, values(actor) as actor, min(_time) as deployed_at_epoch, max(rollout) as rollout by revision | eval deployed_at=strftime(deployed_at_epoch, "%Y-%m-%dT%H:%M:%SZ") | table revision, deployed_at, commit_message, build_id, actor, rollout | sort -deployed_at

source_rows.commit_message: chore: migrate service account to least-privilege SA; remove legacy editor binding

specialists.summary: Deployment Investigator surfaced 2 critical findings addressing: what changed.

spec.name: access
spec.title: Access / Security Investigator
spec.goal: Identify permission and policy events that may explain or contextualize the incident.
spec.lead_question: Who can do what, and did that change?
sub_questions: What IAM binding changes landed in or shortly before the incident window?
Which principals are seeing permission denials, on which resources?
Do any policy changes (SetIamPolicy) line up with resources later denied?
Is the affected principal the same one whose binding was changed?

query_repertoire.name: access_iam_changes_in_window
query_repertoire.purpose: IAM binding changes (roles added/removed) recorded in the incident window.
query_repertoire.spl: search index=iam_changes sourcetype=iam_binding_change | eval change_time=strftime(_time, "%Y-%m-%dT%H:%M:%SZ") | table change_time, action, role, member, resource, actor, change_reason | sort -change_time

query_repertoire.name: access_audit_denials_by_principal
query_repertoire.purpose: Audit-log permission-denied events grouped by the principal that was denied, in the incident window.
query_repertoire.spl: search index=cloud_audit sourcetype=cloud_audit status=PERMISSION_DENIED | stats count as denials, values(method) as methods, values(resource) as resources, min(_time) as first_seen_epoch, max(_time) as last_seen_epoch by principal_email | eval first_denied=strftime(first_seen_epoch, "%Y-%m-%dT%H:%M:%SZ") | eval last_denied=strftime(last_seen_epoch, "%Y-%m-%dT%H:%M:%SZ") | table principal_email, denials, methods, resources, first_denied, last_denied | sort -denials

query_repertoire.name: access_policy_changes_for_affected_resources
query_repertoire.purpose: SetIamPolicy / policy mutations affecting the resources that later showed permission denials.
query_repertoire.spl: search index=cloud_audit sourcetype=cloud_audit method=SetIamPolicy | eval change_time=strftime(_time, "%Y-%m-%dT%H:%M:%SZ") | table change_time, resource, principal_email, delta, request_id | sort -change_time

findings.claim: 583 PERMISSION_DENIED audit events on secretmanager.versions.access, all from the checkout-api runtime SA.
findings.source_query_spl: search index=cloud_audit sourcetype=cloud_audit status=PERMISSION_DENIED | stats count as denials, values(method) as methods, values(resource) as resources, min(_time) as first_seen_epoch, max(_time) as last_seen_epoch by principal_email | eval first_denied=strftime(first_seen_epoch, "%Y-%m-%dT%H:%M:%SZ") | eval last_denied=strftime(last_seen_epoch, "%Y-%m-%dT%H:%M:%SZ") | table principal_email, denials, methods, resources, first_denied, last_denied | sort -denials

findings.claim: roles/secretmanager.secretAccessor was REMOVED from the checkout-api SA on checkout-stripe-key at 14:01:12 — 3s before the deploy.
findings.source_query_spl: search index=iam_changes sourcetype=iam_binding_change | eval change_time=strftime(_time, "%Y-%m-%dT%H:%M:%SZ") | table change_time, action, role, member, resource, actor, change_reason | sort -change_time

specialists.summary: Access / Security Investigator surfaced 2 critical findings addressing: who can do what, and did that change.

spec.name: blast_radius
spec.title: Blast Radius Investigator
spec.goal: Establish the scope of impact — who and what is affected by the failure.
sub_questions: Which tenants/customers are seeing the failure, and at what rate?
Which endpoints carry the failure vs which remain healthy?
Are downstream services seeing knock-on errors?
Is the failure regional or global?

query_repertoire.name: blast_radius_error_share_by_tenant
query_repertoire.purpose: Are all tenants affected equally, or is this concentrated in a subset?
query_repertoire.spl: search index=app_logs sourcetype=access_log service=$service$ tenant=* | stats count as requests, count(eval(status>=500)) as errors by tenant | eval error_rate_pct=round(errors*100.0/requests, 2) | sort -error_rate_pct

query_repertoire.name: blast_radius_error_share_by_endpoint
query_repertoire.purpose: Which endpoints carry the failure? Healthy slices (e.g. /healthz) help bound the blast.
query_repertoire.spl: search index=app_logs sourcetype=access_log service=$service$ | stats count as requests, count(eval(status>=500)) as errors by endpoint | eval error_rate_pct=round(errors*100.0/requests, 2) | sort -error_rate_pct

query_repertoire.name: blast_radius_downstream_impact
query_repertoire.purpose: Are downstream services seeing knock-on errors? If so, the blast extends past the origin.
query_repertoire.spl: search index=app_logs sourcetype=access_log upstream=$service$ | stats count as requests, count(eval(status>=500)) as errors by service | eval error_rate_pct=round(errors*100.0/requests, 2) | sort -errors

query_repertoire.name: blast_radius_region_share
query_repertoire.purpose: Is one region affected or all of them? Distinguishes regional outage from global failure.
query_repertoire.spl: search index=app_logs sourcetype=access_log service=$service$ region=* | stats count as requests, count(eval(status>=500)) as errors by region | eval error_rate_pct=round(errors*100.0/requests, 2) | sort -error_rate_pct

findings.claim: Checkout write endpoints fail at ~21% while GET /orders sits at ~0.5% — the fault tracks the checkout write path.
findings.source_query_spl: search index=app_logs sourcetype=access_log service=checkout-api | stats count as requests, count(eval(status>=500)) as errors by endpoint | eval error_rate_pct=round(errors*100.0/requests, 2) | sort -error_rate_pct

findings.claim: Three downstream services (order-fulfillment, notification, payment-gateway) error at ~100% in sympathy with checkout-api.
findings.source_query_spl: search index=app_logs sourcetype=access_log upstream=checkout-api | stats count as requests, count(eval(status>=500)) as errors by service | eval error_rate_pct=round(errors*100.0/requests, 2) | sort -errors

findings.claim: Failure is confined to us-central1 (~27%); us-east1 and eu-west1 stay under 0.5% — consistent with the 00042 rollout.
findings.source_query_spl: search index=app_logs sourcetype=access_log service=checkout-api region=* | stats count as requests, count(eval(status>=500)) as errors by region | eval error_rate_pct=round(errors*100.0/requests, 2) | sort -error_rate_pct

specialists.summary: Blast Radius Investigator surfaced 3 critical findings addressing: who and what is affected.

shared_evidence.text: All four specialists — reliability, deployment, access, blast radius — converged on revision `checkout-api-00042` in the same window.
